label: Japanese Streetwear
description: Oversized layered silhouettes with baggy trousers, elongated tees, and deconstructed outerwear in muted earth tones and blacks, often paired with chunky sneakers

label: Korean Minimalism
description: Clean-cut tailoring with slim-fit trousers, oversized blazers in cream, beige, and charcoal, structured silhouettes with almost no visible branding

label: Gorpcore
description: Technical outdoor gear worn as fashion: Arc'teryx shells, Salomon trail runners, cargo pants with multiple utility pockets, fleece vests, and hiking boots

label: Old Money
description: Preppy east-coast aesthetic with polo shirts, cable-knit sweaters, khaki chinos, loafers, and navy blazers in muted heritage colors suggesting generational wealth

label: Y2K Revival
description: Early 2000s nostalgia: low-rise jeans, baby tees, velour tracksuits, butterfly clips, metallic fabrics, and platform shoes in hot pink, chrome silver, and sky blue

label: Techwear
description: Functional futuristic aesthetic with waterproof shells, modular cargo systems, cyberpunk silhouettes in all-black palette, with visible utility straps and zippers

label: Dark Academia
description: Gothic scholarly aesthetic with tweed blazers, turtlenecks, plaid trousers, Oxford shoes, and leather satchels in deep burgundy, forest green, brown, and black

label: Cottagecore
description: Rural romanticism with flowy floral dresses, linen pinafores, puffed sleeves, straw hats, and wicker baskets in soft pastels and warm naturals

label: Streetwear Luxury
description: High-low mix of streetwear silhouettes with luxury execution: hoodies and sweatpants in cashmere, logo-heavy pieces from Supreme x Louis Vuitton collabs, designer sneakers

label: Coastal Grandmother
description: Relaxed seaside elegance with linen trousers, oversized striped shirts, canvas totes, woven sandals, and sun hats in navy, white, cream, and sandy beige

label: Workwear Americana
description: Blue-collar American heritage: raw selvedge denim, Carhartt canvas duck jackets, Red Wing boots, chambray shirts, and trucker hats in indigo, tan, and olive

label: Soft Grunge
description: Feminine take on 90s grunge: floral dresses over ripped fishnet tights, band tees knotted at the waist, chunky boots, denim jackets with patches in faded black and dusty rose

label: Ivy League Preppy
description: Classic American campus style: varsity jackets, madras plaid shorts, crewneck sweaters tied over shoulders, penny loafers, and boat shoes in red, navy, and cream

label: Normcore
description: Deliberately average, anti-fashion aesthetic: plain white tees, straight-leg jeans, dad sneakers, zip-up fleece, and baseball caps in neutral basics

label: Quiet Luxury
description: Logoless understated wealth: cashmere turtlenecks, perfectly tailored trousers, camel coats, leather goods, in muted neutrals of camel, navy, cream, and charcoal

label: Afropunk
description: Bold fusion of African heritage with punk rebellion: natural afros adorned with Ankara prints, leather jackets, spiked accessories, and vibrant color clashing

label: E-Girl
description: TikTok-era alternative aesthetic: plaid mini skirts layered over long-sleeve striped shirts, chain necklaces, heart face stickers, dyed hair, and platform shoes

label: Boho Chic
description: Free-spirited bohemian: crochet tops, peasant blouses, maxi skirts, fringe vests, tassel jewelry, and suede ankle boots in terracotta, cream, and warm brown

label: Military Surplus
description: Utilitarian army aesthetic: M65 field jackets, cargo pants with numerous pockets, combat boots, dog tags, and camouflage prints in olive drab, khaki, and camo

label: Avant-Garde Fashion
description: Conceptual anti-conventional clothing: deconstructed seams, asymmetric hemlines, architectural silhouettes, unexpected material combinations pushing the boundary of wearability

label: Skatecore
description: Skateboarding subculture style: loose graphic tees, baggy Dickies or Levi's, Vans slip-ons, bucket hats, and hoodies with skate brand logos in faded and worn finishes

label: Punk Revival
description: Neo-punk rebellion: studded leather jackets, torn fishnet, tartan bondage trousers, safety-pin accessories, mohawks, and band tees in black with neon accents

label: Athleisure
description: Performance sportswear worn as everyday fashion: sleek leggings, moisture-wicking tops, track jackets, and seamless sets in monochrome or bold athletic colorways

label: Western Cowboy
description: American frontier revival: Stetson cowboy hats, bootcut denim, embroidered snap-button western shirts, turquoise jewelry, and pointed-toe cowboy boots

label: Subversive Basics
description: Elevated everyday pieces with unexpected twists: deconstructed white shirts, inside-out seams on plain sweaters, raw-edge hemlines on trousers, minimal but intentional

label: Regencycore
description: Bridgerton-inspired Regency Era romanticism: empire-waist gowns, puffed sleeves, corsets worn over blouses, pearl accessories, and pastel floral prints

label: Barbiecore
description: Hyper-feminine maximalist pink: hot pink mini dresses, all-pink monochromatic outfits, platform heels, furry accessories, rhinestone embellishments, and Barbie-doll aesthetics

label: Coastal Surf
description: Beach culture aesthetic: boardshorts, rashguards, sun-bleached tees, Birkenstock sandals, and woven bucket hats in ocean blues, coral, and sandy tan

label: Hipster Indie
description: Counter-culture thrift aesthetic: vintage flannel shirts, high-waisted mom jeans, round wire-frame glasses, canvas tote bags, and worn leather Oxford shoes

label: Maximalist Glam
description: More-is-more high fashion opulence: bold pattern mixing, sequins, feathers, jewel tones, layered statement jewelry, and dramatic silhouettes in crimson, gold, and emerald

label: Quiet Streetwear
description: Logoless premium streetwear: heavyweight blank hoodies, straight-leg denim, clean white sneakers, and minimal outerwear in neutral grays, blacks, and off-whites

label: Festival Fashion
description: Music festival dressing: crochet bralettes, fringe shorts, glitter body paint, flower crowns, holographic accessories, and bold prints in neon and metallic rainbow

label: Vintage Sportswear
description: Retro athletic wear: 80s nylon track suits, vintage NBA jerseys, classic Adidas Samba and Nike Cortez, color-blocked windbreakers in primary colors and gold

label: Romantic Goth
description: Victorian darkness meets romance: black lace corsets, velvet capes, cameo brooches, fishnet sleeves, and platform Mary Janes in deep purples, black, and blood red

label: Tropical Resort
description: Vacation luxury with bold tropical prints, silk resort shirts, wide-leg linen trousers, espadrille wedges, and oversized straw hats in turquoise, coral, and banana yellow

label: Archival Fashion
description: Rare vintage designer pieces worn as cultural artifacts: early 2000s Dior by Galliano, 90s Helmut Lang, vintage Versace prints, treating fashion as collectible art

label: Gender-Fluid Fashion
description: Deliberately non-binary dressing: men in skirts, women in boxy suiting, mixing traditionally gendered garments to create ambiguous powerful silhouettes

label: Indie Sleaze
description: 2008-2012 era underground nightlife aesthetic: American Apparel basics, vintage Ray-Bans, leather jackets, smudged eye makeup, and a deliberately disheveled party look

label: Mob Wife Aesthetic
description: Extravagant 80s-90s Italian-American opulence: full-length fur coats, animal prints, gold jewelry layering, tight bodycon dresses, and big hair in dramatic jewel tones

label: Minimalist Scandinavian
description: Nordic restraint with perfectly proportioned pieces: structured coats, clean-lined trousers, simple knitwear, and quality leather goods in white, grey, cream, and black

label: Twee
description: Whimsical indie cuteness: Peter Pan collars, A-line skirts, Mary Jane shoes, cardigans with embroidered patches, and quirky cat-eye glasses in pastel and candy colors

label: Biker & Moto
description: Motorcycle subculture style: asymmetric zip leather jackets, skinny black jeans, engineer boots, bandanas, and graphic tees in all-black with chrome hardware accents

label: 90s Hip-Hop
description: Golden era rap aesthetics: oversized Timberlands, baggy denim, cross-color jeans, Coogi sweaters, doorknob chains, Kangol hats, and tracksuits in bold primary colors

label: Clean Girl
description: Effortless minimal polish: slicked-back bun, gold hoop earrings, ribbed tank tops, straight-leg denim, ballet flats, and dewy skin in neutral earth tones

label: Denim on Denim
description: Canadian tuxedo maximized: double denim from head to toe in various washes — dark jacket over light jeans or matching indigo sets, a deliberate fashion statement

label: Print Mixing
description: Intentional clashing of patterns: stripes with florals, plaids with animal prints, deliberately mismatched but balanced through color family or scale variation

label: Punk Couture
description: High fashion interpretation of punk: McQueen tartan, Westwood corsets, Givenchy studded leather, safety-pin evening gowns, transforming subversion into luxury runway pieces

label: Tailored Suiting
description: Power dressing with immaculate construction: single-button suits, peak lapels, double-breasted blazers, French-cuffed shirts, and pocket squares in charcoal, navy, and chalk stripe

label: Logomania
description: Visible luxury branding as status signal: monogram canvas bags, all-over logo prints, branded belts, and head-to-toe designer labeling as deliberate display of affluence

label: Distressed & Grunge
description: Deliberately destroyed finishes: shredded denim, acid-washed hoodies, bleach-splattered tees, paint-stained jeans, and worn-out Converse in faded blacks and grays

label: Cyberpunk Fashion
description: Dystopian futurism: reflective materials, LED-embedded garments, PVC outerwear, neon color blocking, and asymmetric cuts suggesting near-future urban warfare

label: Urban Outdoor
description: City-ready outdoor performance gear: Patagonia vests over button-downs, puffer jackets, chino pants, and hiking-inspired sneakers blending function with office-appropriate polish

label: Golden Hour Glow
description: Warm luminous palette inspired by late afternoon light: amber, burnt sienna, mustard yellow, rust orange, and cream fabrics in flowing silhouettes with natural texture

label: Monochromatic Dressing
description: Head-to-toe single color in varied tones and textures: all-black with matte and glossy finishes, all-white mixing sheer and opaque, creating depth through material contrast

label: Abstract Expressionism
description: Large-scale gestural mark-making with drips, splashes, and bold brush strokes in raw emotional color, suggesting movement and psychological depth

label: Brutalist Architecture
description: Raw exposed concrete structures with massive geometric forms, repetitive modular grids, and unfinished textural surfaces conveying monumental weight and permanence

label: Pop Art
description: Commercial imagery elevated to fine art: bold Ben-Day dots, primary colors, flat graphic style, celebrity portraits, and consumer product iconography

label: Japanese Woodblock
description: Ukiyo-e flat graphic style with bold outlines, limited color palette, stylized waves and nature scenes, and decorative pattern-filling in the tradition of the floating world

label: Bauhaus Design
description: Form follows function geometric precision: primary colors, geometric sans-serif typography, horizontal-vertical composition, and the marriage of fine art with industrial craft

label: Art Nouveau
description: Organic curvilinear forms inspired by nature: flowing floral motifs, sinuous female figures, stained glass color effects, and whiplash curves in gold and emerald

label: Surrealism
description: Dreamlike juxtapositions with hyperrealist rendering of impossible scenes, melting forms, disembodied body parts, and subconscious imagery in meticulous photorealistic detail

label: Minimalist Art
description: Reduction to essential form: monochrome canvases, geometric solids, industrial materials, serial repetition, and the elimination of personal expression in favor of pure concept

label: Street Art & Graffiti
description: Urban mark-making: wildstyle lettering, character illustration, wheat-paste posters, and stencil-spray works in high-contrast colors on architectural surfaces

label: Impressionism
description: Capturing fleeting light effects with visible broken brushstrokes, soft edges, and atmospheric color — garden scenes, water surfaces, café life in dappled natural light

label: Digital Glitch Art
description: Intentional digital corruption: pixel sorting, data moshing, RGB channel separation, scan-line distortion, and JPEG artifact aesthetics celebrating technological failure

label: Hyperrealism
description: Photographic precision in paint or sculpture: skin pores, fabric wrinkles, water droplets, and reflective surfaces rendered with microscopic accuracy surpassing photographic detail

label: Psychedelic Art
description: Mind-altering visual imagery: kaleidoscopic patterns, melting symmetrical forms, neon color saturation, morphing organic shapes, and visual vibration suggesting altered consciousness

label: Architectural Photography
description: Geometric urban documentation: extreme perspective convergence, leading lines into vanishing points, shadow and light on textural surfaces, and abstract building facade patterns

label: Vintage Photography
description: Analog photographic aesthetics: film grain, light leaks, faded color palettes, lomographic vignetting, expired film color shifts, and Polaroid border textures

label: Fashion Photography
description: Editorial image-making emphasizing garment, light, and concept: dramatic studio lighting, location storytelling, post-processing color grading, and model as canvas

label: Concept Art
description: Pre-production visual development for film, games, and media: atmospheric environment paintings, character design sheets, and mood boards establishing visual world-building

label: Fine Art Portraiture
description: Psychological character study through controlled light and pose: Rembrandt lighting, Old Master chiaroscuro, psychological tension in the gaze, and painterly skin texture

label: Color Field Painting
description: Large expanses of flat unmodulated color creating emotional atmosphere through hue relationships: soft edges, atmospheric staining, and color as primary expressive vehicle

label: Neo-Expressionism
description: Return to figurative raw paint handling: crude mark-making, violent impasto, distorted figures, and primal emotional urgency in thick gestural paint application

label: Textile & Surface Pattern
description: Decorative repeat pattern design: intricate geometric tessellation, botanical repeats, folk motifs, and ornamental surface design in rich layered color

label: Neon Signage & Light Art
description: Glowing bent-glass tube lettering and sculpture: vivid saturated color bleeding into surrounding space, retro Americana diner signs, and contemporary neon art installations

label: Natural Landscape
description: Wilderness and terrain documentation: vast scale contrast between human and nature, dramatic sky and weather, golden hour topography, and untouched ecological beauty

label: Brutalist Photography
description: Stark documentary realism: harsh direct flash, desaturated color, unflattering angles, social realism subject matter, and anti-aesthetic compositional choices

label: Contemporary Still Life
description: Arranged object studies with controlled studio light: dramatic chiaroscuro on ceramic and glass, dewy fruit textures, and symbolic object groupings in painterly or photographic form

label: Kinetic & Interactive Art
description: Movement-based and participatory works: motorized sculpture, wind-driven mobiles, light responsive installations, and audience-activated environments that change in real time

label: Editorial Illustration
description: Concept-driven image-making for print: bold graphic metaphor, limited color palette, wit and conceptual economy translating complex ideas into single compelling images

label: Urban Decay & Ruins
description: Abandoned and deteriorating architecture: peeling paint revealing layered histories, rusted industrial equipment, overgrown interiors reclaimed by nature, and beautiful entropy

label: Celestial & Cosmic
description: Astronomical imagery and cosmic scale: nebula color palettes, star field depth, planetary surface textures, and the overwhelming vastness of deep space rendered in saturated blues and golds

label: Botanical Illustration
description: Scientific plant documentation elevated to fine art: precise specimen rendering, cross-section anatomy, delicate watercolor washes on cream paper, and taxonomic labeling

label: Analog Collage & Cut-Up
description: Physical paper cutting and reassembly: found image fragments, magazine ephemera, unexpected spatial juxtapositions, visible tape and glue edges, and tactile material evidence

label: Wabi-Sabi
description: Japanese aesthetic of imperfect impermanence: cracked ceramic glazes, weathered wood grain, asymmetric handmade vessels, mossy stone, and the beauty of age and incompleteness

label: Retrofuturism
description: Past visions of the future: Space Age Googie architecture, chrome and pastel interiors, atomic age optimism, flying cars, and moon colony aesthetics of the 1950s-60s

label: Maximalist Interior
description: Layered interior environments: pattern-on-pattern wallpaper, clustered gallery walls, jewel-toned velvet furniture, curated collections of objects, and deliberate visual abundance

label: Wearable Art
description: Garments conceived as sculptural fine art objects: 3D-printed bodices, wire armature dresses, paper and unconventional material clothing, and conceptual pieces prioritizing idea over wearability

label: Typographic Design
description: Typography as visual art: experimental lettering, expressive font pairing, kinetic type, hand-lettered script, and the arrangement of text as primary compositional element

label: Vernacular Photography
description: Amateur and everyday photography elevated: family snapshots, photo booth strips, holiday slides, school portraits, and the authentic unposed humanity of non-art photography

label: Industrial Aesthetic
description: Raw manufacturing materials as design: exposed steel beams, concrete floors, Edison bulb lighting, factory window grids, and the beauty of unfinished industrial processes

label: Organic & Biomorphic Forms
description: Nature-derived abstract forms: sculptural objects resembling organs, seeds, shells, and cellular structures in flowing smooth surfaces suggesting biological growth

label: Grunge & Distressed Texture
description: Surface decay as aesthetic: scratched film, crumpled paper, peeling posters, concrete staining, layered urban texture, and intentional degradation of pristine surfaces
